proven.claim: Early return at line 275 exits when changes is empty
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 298
; Early return at line 275 exits when changes is empty
; Prove: reaching this log requires changes.length > 0

(declare-const changes_length Int)
(declare-const returned_early Int)   ; 1 = early return at line 275 fired
(declare-const line_298_executes Int)

; Transition: early return fires iff changes_length == 0
(assert (>= changes_length 0))
(assert (=> (= changes_length 0) (= returned_early 1)))
; Transition: this log is only reachable if early return did not fire
(assert (=> (= line_298_executes 1) (= returned_early 0)))

; Attempt to falsify: per-change log runs with empty changes
(assert (= line_298_executes 1))
(assert (= changes_length 0))
(check-sat)

violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 298
; sym = symbols[change.type] || "?"
; symbols defines exactly: added, removed, regressed, fixed, unchanged
; An unrecognised type produces sym = "?" — semantically meaningless in output

(declare-const change_type_in_symbols Int)  ; 1 = one of 5 defined types
(declare-const sym_meaningful Int)          ; 1 = sym is one of +/-/!/~/space

; Transition: lookup returns undefined for unknown type → undefined || "?" = "?"
(assert (=> (= change_type_in_symbols 1) (= sym_meaningful 1)))
(assert (=> (= change_type_in_symbols 0) (= sym_meaningful 0)))

; Transition: runDiff does not validate change.type before indexing into symbols
; diffAgainst return value is unconstrained with respect to the 5 defined types
(assert (= change_type_in_symbols 0))
(assert (= sym_meaningful 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: change.claim.slice(0, 70): if claim == "", result is "" — no crash, but no content
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 298
; change.claim.slice(0, 70): if claim == "", result is "" — no crash, but no content
; Output: "  + file:line  LABEL:  [principle]" — claim section invisible

(declare-const claim_length Int)
(declare-const slice_length Int)

; Transition: String.prototype.slice(0, 70) returns first min(len, 70) chars
(assert (>= claim_length 0))
(assert (>= slice_length 0))
(assert (<= slice_length 70))
(assert (<= slice_length claim_length))
(assert (=> (< claim_length 70) (= slice_length claim_length)))
(assert (=> (>= claim_length 70) (= slice_length 70)))

; Boundary: diffAgainst returns a change where claim is the empty string
(assert (= claim_length 0))
(assert (= slice_length 0))
(check-sat)
